source: Liao, Z., Nan, Y., Liang, H., Hao, S., Zhai, J., Wu, J., Zheng, Z. — "SmartAxe: Detecting Cross-Chain Vulnerabilities in Bridge Smart Contracts via Fine-Grained Static Analysis", FSE 2024 (Proc. ACM Softw. Eng., Vol. 1, FSE, Article 12).
methodology_note: Per SmartAxe paper §6 the manual eval set is '16 vulnerable cross-chain bridge applications (203 smart contracts) drawn from 20 real CCV attacks'. The paper reports aggregate metrics (precision 84.95%, recall 89.77%) but does not enumerate the 16 bridges in a single table within the publicly accessible HTML/PDF, and the figshare artifact is access-controlled (HTTP 403). Only PolyNetwork, ChainSwap and ThORChain are named explicitly as motivating examples. To avoid overclaiming we mark every per-bridge cell null with notes; if the artifact becomes accessible later the file can be updated. SmartAxe is static analysis — when populated, tte_seconds should hold per-contract analysis wall-clock, not fuzz-style time-to-find.

polynetwork.note: Named in §1 of paper as a $600M access-control failure. Likely in the 16-bridge eval set, but per-bridge detection not published — leaving null until verified from artifact.

wormhole.note: not enumerated in publicly accessible paper materials

_instructions_for_filling: Filled 2026-04-28 from the publicly accessible HTML render of arXiv 2406.15999 + the SmartAxe FSE 2024 paper preprint at https://people.cs.umass.edu/~juanzhai/papers/fse24liao.pdf. The figshare artifact (https://figshare.com/articles/code/FSE24-SmartAxe_artifact/24218808) returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch, so the dataset's specific 16-bridge enumeration could not be cross-checked. Update individual cells when artifact access is restored or when the paper authors publish a per-bridge breakdown.
